steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: "Main purpose" questions ask what the passage as a whole is doing, not just what a single sentence says. The passage shows Celestine at work with her infant daughter, describing physical moments of connection and ending with an explicit statement that excitement runs through every hour despite her exhaustion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To show that Celestine enjoys imagining her daughter's future life.: The passage shows Celestine reacting to her daughter in the present moment (catching her gaze, picking her up). There is no imagining of the future. ✗
  • Choice B — To explain how Celestine's coworkers bond with her daughter.: No coworkers appear in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — To portray Celestine's excitement about her daughter.: The passage builds toward this in every detail — her breath catching, dropping her tools, anticipating her daughter’s first word — and the final sentence states it outright: "there was a nerve of excitement running through each hour." ✓
  • Choice D — To imply that Celestine is dissatisfied with her job.: Exhaustion is mentioned only to be contrasted with excitement. The passage does not suggest dissatisfaction with the job itself. ✗
That is the correct answer.
